page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-2 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel processor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading, resulting in 2 threads per core. The memory configuration consists of 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The CPU includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, alongside advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data indicates moderate single-core capabilities but limited multi-core and memory throughput. The single-core performance is average, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1640.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1554.59. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the weak to poor ranges. For instance, the stress-ng multi-core score is 1600.79, and uncached large-block memory read bandwidth is measured at 15.05 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET performance are also low, with Redis achieving 2.24 million operations per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the n4-highmem-2 offers a high memory density relative to its low vCPU count, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require intense parallel processing. Given the poor multi-core scaling and weak database performance, it is not suited for heavy database engines or high-throughput web serving. Instead, it is best utilized for lightweight memory-intensive tasks, single-threaded applications, basic text processing, or small-scale caching layers where RAM capacity is prioritized over raw compute power.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-2 is an x86_64 server featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs from 1 physical Intel core and 16.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512. The instance does not include bundled local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show average single-core execution, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1640.0, but weak multi-core and memory bandwidth results, such as a stress-ng multi-core score of 1600.79. Qualitatively, this server offers a cost-effective entry point for memory-heavy workloads that do not demand high parallel processing power. It is best suited for lightweight memory-intensive applications, single-threaded tasks, basic text processing, and small-scale caching layers.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-2 is an x86_64 server with 2 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 16 GB RAM. It offers a high memory-to-core ratio for lightweight, memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-2 is an x86_64 server featuring 2 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and average single-core performance.
tagline: This low vCPU instance provides high memory density and advanced Intel instruction sets for lightweight workloads that prioritize memory capacity over raw compute power.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated vCPUs on 1 physical Intel core with hyper-threading
16.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction set support
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for lightweight memory-intensive and single-threaded tasks
